Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is mildly impaired (elevated serum creatinine). Urinalysis shows significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Low urine specific gravity suggests impaired concentrating ability. HbA1c is low, indicating no chronic hyperglycemia. Findings suggest possible acute or chronic kidney dysfunction with protein loss and abnormal urine findings; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
